Comparison Report
Sinapsis vs Cortex
Overview At a Glance Architecture Feature Comparison What Each Does Better Improvement Ideas Credits
Data sourced from FEATURES.md of both repositories. Report generated 2026-04-13. No affiliation between projects.
Honest & Balanced Comparison — April 2026

Sinapsis v4.3.3 vs Cortex v3.11.1

Published 2026-04-13 — Data verified from each project's FEATURES.md

Two independent approaches to the same problem: teaching Claude Code to learn from its operator over time. Both ship hooks, pattern detectors, dream cycles, and confidence management — but make very different trade-off choices. This report presents the facts without favoritism.

Luispitik/sinapsis fermonterom/fs-cortex

At a Glance

Key numbers from each project, side by side.

Sinapsis v4.3.3

Commands 15
Hook files 7
Core lines 1,614
Tests 83
Test suites 6
Skills shipped 3
Token budget 6,915/session
Agents 0
License MIT
Scrubbing patterns 8
Pattern detectors 5
CI platforms Ubuntu/macOS/Win

Cortex v3.11.1

Commands 17
Hooks + Lib modules 4 + 5
Core lines —
Tests 159
Test suites 12
Skills shipped 1
Token budget 8,000/session
Agents 3
License MIT
Scrubbing patterns 12
Pattern detectors 5
CI platforms Ubuntu/macOS/Win

Architecture

How each system processes observations into actionable knowledge.

Sinapsis Pipeline

1
observe_v3.py
PostToolUse hook · 200 lines · flags is_error
2
_session-learner.sh
Stop hook · 329 lines · 5 pattern detectors
3
_instinct-activator.sh
PreToolUse hook · 221 lines · occurrence tracking + auto-promote
4
_passive-activator.sh
PreToolUse hook · 66 lines · rule engine (6 rules)
5
_project-context.sh
PreToolUse hook · 136 lines · project state injection
6
_dream.sh
Scheduled command · 501 lines · index hygiene + decay
7
_eod-gather.sh
End-of-day summary · 161 lines · multi-project

Cortex Pipeline

1
observe.py
PostToolUse hook · observation capture
2
session-learner.js
Stop hook · 5 pattern detectors · Node.js runtime
3
injector.sh → injector-engine.js
PreToolUse hook · token budget 8000 · reflexes exempt
4
session-start.py
Notification hook · reset session token counter
5
dream_cycle.py
Lib module · confidence decay + hygiene + law distillation
6
validate_instinct.py + yaml-utils.js
Lib modules · YAML import validation + multiline safety
7
cortex_utils.py
Lib module · shared utilities, HOME/CWD validation

Feature Comparison

Category-by-category breakdown. Badges indicate where one system has a measurable advantage.

Feature Sinapsis v4.3.3 Cortex v3.11.1
Knowledge Management
Confidence model 3 discrete levels: draft / confirmed / permanent. Simple, predictable. Continuous 0.00–0.95. More granular ranking. edge
Auto-promote 5+ occurrences AND 3+ sessions → draft→confirmed 5+ activations AND 3+ sessions → 0.35. Same threshold logic.
Decay mechanism Inline write at dream. confirmed 60d→draft, draft 90d→archived. Permanent exempt. Read-only skip at injection (60d). Dream writes -0.05/30d linear. edge
Storage format Single JSON index for all instincts. Compact. Fast reads. Individual YAML per instinct. Git-friendly diffs. edge
Law distillation Not implemented. Instincts at 0.90+ auto-distill to TXT one-liners (max 10 laws). unique
Cross-project promotion Manual via /promote command. Automatic: Jaccard ≥ 0.70 + 2 projects + avg conf ≥ 0.60 → global. edge
Operator State Strategic decisions JSON. Cross-project memory with retired tech tracking. unique Not implemented.
Knowledge timeline Not implemented. Append-only knowledge-log.md with 11 event types. unique
Token budget 1,500 chars/tool-use (per injection). 6,915 tok/session across 3 skills. edge 8,000 tok/session total budget. Reflexes exempt from budget. Higher ceiling.
Security
Secret scrubbing 8 patterns: API keys, JWT, PEM, GitHub, AWS, Stripe, Slack, SendGrid 12 patterns: adds SSH, connection strings, Google, Anthropic, OpenAI. edge
Prompt injection 10 blocked keywords + 500 char cap on instinct content. 10 blocked keywords in both Python and JS runtimes. tie
Path traversal Explicit path traversal protection. tie HOME + CWD validation. Per-user temp dirs.
Import safety Not documented as explicit feature. YAML multiline instinct import validation. edge
ReDoS protection Explicit ReDoS-safe regex patterns. edge Not documented as explicit feature.
Process isolation execFileSync (no shell). Atomic writes. Dream lock. edge Hook payload via temp file. Per-user temp dirs. CORTEX-MANAGED markers.
Uninstall safety Not documented. Uninstall safety guard to prevent accidental removal. edge
Pattern Detection
Error-fix Detects error → resolution pairs. tie Detects error → resolution pairs.
Corrections User-correction detection. 3+ edits in overlapping code regions. edge
Workflow chains Trigram-based chain detection. Trigram-based, 3+ occurrences to fire. tie
Repetitions 3+ sessions threshold. 5+ same tool+input threshold. Stricter. edge
Agent patterns Agent pattern detection in session-learner. Jaccard 0.40 similarity + agent evolution tracking. edge
Testing & CI
Test count 83 tests across 6 suites. 159 tests across 12 suites. edge
Lint integration Pre-push hook. ShellCheck + flake8 lint in CI. Dedicated lint job. edge
CI matrix Ubuntu + macOS + Windows. fail-fast: false. tie 2×2×2 matrix: OS × Python × Node. Badge job.
PowerShell tests Windows CI via bash. No native PowerShell. 1 dedicated PowerShell test suite. edge
Portability
Backup /backup command with cloud-agnostic targets. edge cx-backup to .tar.gz.
Restore /restore from any backup location (OneDrive, GDrive, Dropbox, iCloud, USB). edge cx-restore from .tar.gz archive.
Cleanup /cleanup command for removing stale data. unique Not shipped as dedicated command.
Export Not a dedicated command. cx-export for external sharing. unique
Installer & UX
Installer bash + bat. Legacy cleanup on upgrade. tie bash + PowerShell. Uninstaller with safety guard.
Interactive shorthand Not implemented. A=Accept, X=Reject, S=Skip, M=Merge, O=Omit, I=Install. unique
Skill routing Dedicated skill-router (4,150 tok) for on-demand skill loading. unique cx-router as a command (not a skill). Simpler routing.
Passive rules Separate engine, 6 rules. Independent from instincts. edge 10 reflexes (deterministic rules). Similar concept, more rules.
Multi-agent Not shipped (v5.0-alpha designed only). 3 agents: cortex-observer (Opus 1M), cortex-reviewer (Sonnet ×3), cortex-planner (Sonnet). unique
Validation command Not shipped. cx-validate for instinct integrity checks. unique
Retrospective Not shipped (available as library skill outside core). cx-retro for session retrospectives. edge
Gotcha tracking Not shipped. cx-gotcha for capturing recurring pitfalls. unique

What Each Does Better

Genuine advantages, not marketing. Each project makes trade-offs the other doesn't.

Sinapsis Advantages

1 Operator State
A strategic decisions JSON that persists cross-project memory: retired tech warnings, project blueprints, cross-project lessons. Cortex has no equivalent for operator-level strategic context.
2 Skill Router + Multi-Skill Architecture
3 skills with on-demand loading from a dormant library. The router matches intent to skill tags and installs per-project. Cortex ships a single monolithic skill.
3 Cloud-Agnostic Portability
Backup/restore targets OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox, iCloud, and USB natively. Plus a dedicated /cleanup command. Cortex only backs up to local .tar.gz.
4 Lower Token Overhead
6,915 tokens/session vs 8,000. The per-injection cap of 1,500 chars prevents any single activation from consuming too much context. Every token saved is a token for the user's actual work.
5 Passive Rules Separation
Passive rules (business logic, brand context, tariffs) are kept in a separate engine from learned instincts. This prevents mixing deterministic rules with probabilistic knowledge.
6 Explicit Security Hardening
ReDoS-safe regex, execFileSync (no shell), atomic writes, dream lock, and token budget per injection. Documented and tested security surface.
7 FEATURES.md Reference
Ships a comprehensive feature reference doc alongside code, making the project self-documenting for comparison and audit purposes.

Cortex Advantages

1 Multi-Agent Architecture
3 specialized agents: cortex-observer (Opus 1M for deep analysis), cortex-reviewer (Sonnet x3 for parallel review), cortex-planner (Sonnet for planning). Sinapsis has no agents shipped.
2 Test Coverage Depth
159 tests in 12 suites (nearly 2x Sinapsis), with dedicated ShellCheck + flake8 lint in CI, a PowerShell suite, and a badge generation job. More rigorous validation pipeline.
3 Continuous Confidence + Law Distillation
0.00-0.95 continuous scale gives finer-grained ranking than 3 discrete levels. Instincts at 0.90+ auto-distill into max 10 concise one-liner laws. Elegant knowledge compression.
4 Automatic Cross-Project Promotion
Jaccard similarity >= 0.70 across 2+ projects with avg confidence >= 0.60 triggers automatic promotion to global scope. Sinapsis requires manual /promote.
5 Knowledge Timeline
Append-only knowledge-log.md with 11 event types provides a complete audit trail of how knowledge evolved over time. Full traceability.
6 Broader Secret Scrubbing
12 patterns vs 8. Covers SSH keys, database connection strings, Google tokens, Anthropic keys, and OpenAI keys that Sinapsis does not scrub.
7 Interactive Shorthand + Gotcha Tracking
A/X/S/M/O/I keyboard shortcuts for fast triage. cx-gotcha captures recurring pitfalls as first-class entities. Both reduce operator friction.

Shared Equivalences

Observe → Learn → Inject Both use the same 3-phase pipeline
Dream Cycle Both run periodic index hygiene + confidence decay
5 Pattern Detectors Same five categories detected by both systems
Auto-Promote Threshold Both: 5+ activations AND 3+ sessions
Tri-OS CI Both test on Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows
Backup & Restore Both ship dedicated backup and restore commands
Prompt Injection Block Both block 10 keywords for injection prevention
End-of-Day Summary Both ship /eod or cx-eod for daily wrap-up
Deterministic Rules Passive rules (6) vs Reflexes (10) — same concept

Improvement Ideas

5 ideas for each project, inspired by what the other does well.

For Sinapsis (from Cortex)

Inspired by Cortex
1. Continuous confidence scale
Replace 3 discrete levels with a 0.00-1.00 continuous score. Enables finer-grained injection priority and more nuanced decay curves. Keep the simplicity of named thresholds as aliases (draft=0.10, confirmed=0.35, permanent=0.80).
Inspired by Cortex
2. Law distillation
Auto-distill high-confidence instincts (confirmed 90d+) into concise one-liner laws. Cap at 10 laws for zero-cost injection. Would reduce token overhead further while preserving the most battle-tested knowledge.
Inspired by Cortex
3. Knowledge timeline
Add an append-only event log tracking instinct lifecycle events (created, promoted, decayed, archived, merged). Enables audit trails and retrospective analysis without parsing JSON diffs.
Inspired by Cortex
4. Automatic cross-project promotion
Detect when the same instinct appears across 2+ projects with sufficient confidence and auto-promote to global scope. Replace the current manual /promote with a deterministic Jaccard-based system.
Inspired by Cortex
5. Broader secret scrubbing
Add SSH key, database connection string, Google token, Anthropic key, and OpenAI key patterns to reach 12+ scrubbing rules. Low-effort, high-impact security improvement.

For Cortex (from Sinapsis)

Inspired by Sinapsis
1. Operator State
Add a strategic decisions layer that persists across all projects: retired tech warnings, stack decisions, project blueprints, cross-project lessons. Prevents repeating strategic conversations every session.
Inspired by Sinapsis
2. Multi-skill architecture with router
Split the single cortex skill into domain-specific skills with an intent-matching router. Enables per-project skill installation and reduces token overhead for projects that only need a subset of capabilities.
Inspired by Sinapsis
3. Cloud-agnostic backup targets
Extend cx-backup beyond .tar.gz to support direct backup to cloud storage providers (OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox, iCloud). Reduces friction for non-technical operators who don't work with archives.
Inspired by Sinapsis
4. Per-injection token cap
Add a per-tool-use character limit (e.g. 1,500 chars) alongside the session budget. Prevents any single instinct activation from consuming a disproportionate share of the 8,000 token session budget.
Inspired by Sinapsis
5. Dedicated cleanup command
Ship a cx-cleanup that removes stale observations, orphaned temp files, and expired knowledge artifacts. Complements cx-backup with proactive hygiene rather than just archival.

Sinapsis v4.3.3

Luis Salgado / SalgadoIA

github.com/Luispitik/sinapsis

Cortex v3.11.1

Fernando Montero / Fersora Solutions

github.com/fermonterom/fs-cortex

These two projects evolved independently but tackle the same fundamental challenge: teaching Claude Code to remember what matters. Both have contributed ideas that made the other better. Competition in open ecosystems is a net positive.

Report generated 2026-04-13 · Data from FEATURES.md of both repos · SalgadoIA copper dark theme
